speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Believe me, lords, for flying at the brook

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I saw not better sport these seven years’ day.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet, by your leave, the wind was very high,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And, ten to one, old Joan had not gone out.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But what a point, my lord, your falcon made,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And what a pitch she flew above the rest!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To see how God in all his creatures works!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yea, man and birds are fain of climbing high.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No marvel, an it like your Majesty,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: My Lord Protector’s hawks do tower so well;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: They know their master loves to be aloft

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And bears his thoughts above his falcon’s pitch.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My lord, ’tis but a base ignoble mind

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That mounts no higher than a bird can soar.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I thought as much. He would be above the clouds.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ay, my Lord Cardinal, how think you by that?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Were it not good your Grace could fly to heaven?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The treasury of everlasting joy.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Thy heaven is on Earth; thine eyes and thoughts

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Beat on a crown, the treasure of thy heart.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Pernicious Protector, dangerous peer,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: That smooth’st it so with king and commonweal!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What, cardinal, is your priesthood grown peremptory?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Tantaene animis caelestibus irae?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Churchmen so hot? Good uncle, hide such malice.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With such holiness, can you do it?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No malice, sir, no more than well becomes

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So good a quarrel and so bad a peer.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As who, my lord?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Why, as you, my lord,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: An ’t like your lordly Lord Protectorship.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Why, Suffolk, England knows thine insolence.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And thy ambition, Gloucester.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I prithee peace,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good queen, and whet not on these furious peers,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For blessèd are the peacemakers on Earth.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Let me be blessèd for the peace I make

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Against this proud Protector with my sword!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Faith, holy uncle, would ’t were come to that!

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Marry, when thou

speaker: CARDINAL
text: dar’st!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Make up no factious numbers for the matter.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In thine own person answer thy abuse.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Ay, where thou dar’st not peep. An if thou dar’st,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: This evening, on the east side of the grove.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now, my lords?

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Believe me, cousin Gloucester,

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Had not your man put up the fowl so suddenly,

speaker: CARDINAL
text:
We had had more sport.
                 
                (
                Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Gloucester
                .
                )
              Come with thy two-hand sword.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
True, uncle.
                 
                (
                Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Cardinal
                .
                )
               Are you advised? The east side of the grove.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: I am with you.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, how now, uncle Gloucester?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Talking of hawking; nothing else, my lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
(
                Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Cardinal
                .
                )
                 
              Now, by God’s mother, priest,I’ll shave your crown for this,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or all my fence shall fail.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Medice, teipsum;

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Protector, see to ’t well; protect yourself.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The winds grow high; so do your stomachs, lords.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How irksome is this music to my heart!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When such strings jar, what hope of harmony?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I pray, my lords, let me compound this strife.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What means this noise?—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Fellow, what miracle dost thou proclaim?

speaker: MAN
text: A miracle, a miracle!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Come to the King, and tell him what miracle.

speaker: MAN
text: Forsooth, a blind man at Saint Alban’s shrine

speaker: MAN
text: Within this half hour hath received his sight,

speaker: MAN
text: A man that ne’er saw in his life before.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Now, God be praised, that to believing souls

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Gives light in darkness, comfort in despair.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Here comes the townsmen on procession

speaker: CARDINAL
text: To present your Highness with the man.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Great is his comfort in this earthly vale,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Although by his sight his sin be multiplied.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Stand by, my masters.—Bring him near the King.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His Highness’ pleasure is to talk with him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Good fellow, tell us here the circumstance,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That we for thee may glorify the Lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What, hast thou been long blind and now restored?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Born blind, an ’t please your Grace.

speaker: WIFE
text: Ay, indeed, was he.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: What woman is this?

speaker: WIFE
text: His wife, an ’t like your Worship.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Hadst thou been his mother, thou couldst

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: have better told.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where wert thou born?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: At Berwick in the North, an ’t like your Grace.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Poor soul, God’s goodness hath been great to thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let never day nor night unhallowed pass,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But still remember what the Lord hath done.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Tell me, good fellow, cam’st thou here by chance,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or of devotion to this holy shrine?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: God knows, of pure devotion, being called

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: A hundred times and oftener in my sleep

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: By good Saint Alban, who said “Simon, come,

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Come, offer at my shrine, and I will help thee.”

speaker: WIFE
text: Most true, forsooth, and many time and oft

speaker: WIFE
text: Myself have heard a voice to call him so.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: What, art thou lame?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Ay, God Almighty help me!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How cam’st thou so?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: A fall off of a tree.

speaker: WIFE
text: A plum tree, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: How long hast thou been blind?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: O, born so, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What, and wouldst climb a tree?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: But that in all my life, when I was a youth.

speaker: WIFE
text: Too true, and bought his climbing very dear.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Mass, thou lov’dst plums well, that

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: wouldst venture so.

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Alas, good master, my wife desired some

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: damsons, and made me climb, with danger of my

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: life.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: A subtle knave, but yet it shall not serve.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let me see thine eyes. Wink now. Now open them.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In my opinion, yet thou seest not well.

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Yes, master, clear as day, I thank God and

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Saint Alban.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sayst thou me so? What color is this cloak of?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Red, master, red as blood.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Why, that’s well said. What color is my gown of?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Black, forsooth, coal-black as jet.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, then, thou know’st what color jet is of.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And yet, I think, jet did he never see.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But cloaks and gowns, before this day, a many.

speaker: WIFE
text: Never, before this day, in all his life.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Tell me, sirrah, what’s my name?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Alas, master, I know not.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What’s his name?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: I know not.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Nor his?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: No, indeed, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What’s thine own name?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Sander Simpcox, an if it please you, master.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Then, Sander, sit there, the lying’st knave

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: in Christendom. If thou hadst been born blind,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: thou mightst as well have known all our names as

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: thus to name the several colors we do wear. Sight

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: may distinguish of colors; but suddenly to nominate

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: them all, it is impossible.—My lords, Saint

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Alban here hath done a miracle; and would you

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: not think his cunning to be great that could

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: restore this cripple to his legs again?

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: O master, that you could!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My masters of Saint Albans, have you not

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: beadles in your town and things called whips?

speaker: MAYOR
text: Yes, my lord, if it please your Grace.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Then send for one presently.

speaker: MAYOR
text: Sirrah, go fetch the beadle hither straight.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now fetch me a stool hither by and by.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
One
                 
                brings
                 
                a
                 
                stool
                .
               Now, sirrah, if you mean to

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: save yourself from whipping, leap me over this

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: stool, and run away.

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Alas, master, I am not able to stand alone.

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: You go about to torture me in vain.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Well, sir, we must have you find your

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: legs.—Sirrah beadle, whip him till he leap over

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: that same stool.

speaker: BEADLE
text: I will, my lord.—Come on, sirrah, off with

speaker: BEADLE
text: your doublet quickly.

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: Alas, master, what shall I do? I am not able to

speaker: SIMPCOX
text: stand.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O God, seest Thou this, and bearest so long?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: It made me laugh to see the villain run.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Follow the knave, and take this drab away.

speaker: WIFE
text: Alas, sir, we did it for pure need.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let them be whipped through every market town

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Till they come to Berwick, from whence they came.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Duke Humphrey has done a miracle today.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: True, made the lame to leap and fly away.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But you have done more miracles than I.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You made in a day, my lord, whole towns to fly.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What tidings with our cousin Buckingham?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Such as my heart doth tremble to unfold:

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: A sort of naughty persons, lewdly bent,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Under the countenance and confederacy

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Of Lady Eleanor, the Protector’s wife,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: The ringleader and head of all this rout,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Have practiced dangerously against your state,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Dealing with witches and with conjurers,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Whom we have apprehended in the fact,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Raising up wicked spirits from under ground,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Demanding of King Henry’s life and death

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And other of your Highness’ Privy Council,

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: As more at large your Grace shall understand.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: And so, my Lord Protector, by this means

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Your lady is forthcoming yet at London.

speaker: CARDINAL
text:
Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Gloucester
                .
                 
              This news, I think, hath turned your weapon’s edge;

speaker: CARDINAL
text: ’Tis like, my lord, you will not keep your hour.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Ambitious churchman, leave to afflict my heart.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sorrow and grief have vanquished all my powers,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And, vanquished as I am, I yield to thee,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Or to the meanest groom.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O God, what mischiefs work the wicked ones,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Heaping confusion on their own heads thereby!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Gloucester, see here the tainture of thy nest,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And look thyself be faultless, thou wert best.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Madam, for myself, to heaven I do appeal

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: How I have loved my king and commonweal;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And, for my wife, I know not how it stands.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sorry I am to hear what I have heard.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Noble she is; but if she have forgot

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Honor and virtue, and conversed with such

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As, like to pitch, defile nobility,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I banish her my bed and company

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And give her as a prey to law and shame

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: That hath dishonored Gloucester’s honest name.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Well, for this night we will repose us here.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tomorrow toward London back again,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To look into this business thoroughly,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And call these foul offenders to their answers,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And poise the cause in Justice’ equal scales,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose beam stands sure, whose rightful cause prevails.
